title: Ward identities and orbital magnetization in current density functional theory
authors: Giovanni Vignale, Junren Shi, Di Xiao, Qian Niu
abstract: We revisit the derivation of the orbital magnetization formula for periodic crystals in current density functional theory (CDFT)[1]. Our new derivation computes the linear response of the energy density to a periodic magnetic field in the long-wavelength limit. We unveil a Ward identity which connects the current vertex to the derivative of the Kohn-Sham self-energy. The result of Ref.[1] is confirmed: the orbital magnetization of the interacting solid can be computed exactly (in principle) from the self-consistent eigenfunctions and eigenvalues of the Kohn-Sham equation of CDFT.
summary: This paper provides a rigorous theoretical proof that the modern theory of orbital magnetization is formally exact for interacting systems within Current Density Functional Theory. By deriving a new Ward identity, the authors show that the complex effects of electron-electron interactions are captured by using the self-consistent Kohn-Sham velocity. This confirms that researchers can reliably use KS eigenvalues and eigenfunctions to calculate magnetization in interacting solids.
main_problem: The paper seeks to rigorously derive and validate the orbital magnetization formula for periodic crystals within the framework of Current Density Functional Theory (CDFT), specifically addressing whether the 'modern theory' remains exact for interacting systems.
main_result: The authors prove that the orbital magnetization of an interacting solid can be computed exactly using the self-consistent Kohn-Sham eigenfunctions and eigenvalues, establishing a new Ward identity that connects the current vertex to the derivative of the self-energy.
method: A diagrammatic approach using Green's functions and the Dyson series is employed to compute the linear response of the energy density to a periodic magnetic field in the long-wavelength limit.
model_or_system: The system consists of interacting periodic crystals (solids) with broken time-reversal symmetry, described by the Kohn-Sham Hamiltonian in the CDFT framework.
key_observables: Orbital magnetization, paramagnetic current density, energy density fluctuations, and Berry curvature.
important_parameters: Long-wavelength limit (q to 0), zero and finite temperature, and the exchange-correlation vector potential.
main_assumptions: The derivation assumes the 'parallel transport' gauge and utilizes the principle of nearsightedness to define energy density.
figures_summary: Figure 2 shows a bubble diagram for energy density response; Figure 3 displays a complete set of diagrams for the energy density response; Figure 4 illustrates the Dyson equation and contributions to the linearized self-energy insertion.
paper_structure: The paper reviews the non-interacting theory, identifies technical flaws in previous CDFT derivations, presents a rigorous proof using Ward identities and diagrammatic expansions, and extends the results to finite temperature.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
